---
title: "RaCiNG"
author: "Marcelo Hurtado"
date: "2026-03-25"
output: html_document
---

Load libraries
```{r}
library(dplyr)
source("R/RaCInG_input_generation.R")
source("R/network_generation.R")
source("R/feature_extraction.R")
source("R/Utilities.R")
source("R/Monte_Carlo_Method.R")
source("R/txt_to_csv.R")
source("R/Kernel_Method.R")
source("R/statistical_analysis.R")
source("~/Documents/CellTBool/scripts/src/extract_expression_profiles.R")
```

Load input
```{r}
counts = read.csv("~/Documents/multideconv_paper/input/raw_counts_Mariathasan.csv", row.names = 1)
```

Method A: Monte Carlo
```{r}
set.seed(1)
res_montecarlo = compute_racing_montecarlo(counts, output_folder = "~/Documents/racing/vignettes/", deconv_method = "Quantiseq", file_name = "Test", 
                                           nPatients = 3, communication_type = "W", Ncells = 10, Ngraphs = 2, Ndegree = 2, norm = TRUE, pt_idx = 1)
# res_montecarlo: 3 patients x 286 features
```

Method B: Kernel method
```{r}
res_kernel = compute_racing_kernel(counts, output_folder = "~/Documents/racing/vignettes/", deconv_method = "Quantiseq", 
                                   file_name = "Test", nPatients = "all", communication_type = "W", norm = TRUE, pt_idx = NULL)

# res_kernel: 11 cells x 11 cells x 192 features
```

Statistical analysis
```{r}
results = read.csv("~/Documents/racing/vignettes/SKCM_W_1000_cells_3_deg_data.csv", row.names = 1)
# Suppose you have a vector of group labels
grouping <- rep(c("Group1", "Group2"), 5)  # length must match nrow(results)

# Run the Wilcoxon tests
wilcox_results <- wilcox_group_test(results, grouping)

# View top significant features
head(wilcox_results)

# Assuming wilcox_results is your data frame
volcano_plot(wilcox_results, top_labels = 15, p_threshold = 1, 
                         title = "SKCM Wilcoxon Volcano Plot")
```
